skill.name: research-lookup
skill.description: Search current academic research through OpenRouter using Perplexity Sonar models. The skill selects a fast lookup model or a reasoning model based on query complexity and returns cited research summaries.
skill.summary: Search scholarly sources and return cited research summaries with automatic model selection.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged credential access, network access, file writing, documentation command examples, and weak-crypto patterns. Most high-severity static findings are false positives for a legitimate OpenRouter integration, but the skill intentionally sends user research queries to an external API and can write output to a user-specified file. The documentation also includes behavioral steering toward another skill and a hosted K-Dense product, so publication should include clear external-service and content-steering warnings.

medium_findings.title: User Queries Are Sent to an External Research API
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY from the environment and sends formatted user research queries to the OpenRouter chat completions endpoint. This is core functionality, but users must know that query text and research context leave the local environment. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The code shows a bearer token header and requests.post call to the configured OpenRouter base URL, with user query content embedded in messages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key, authorization header, request body, and outbound request are visible in the main script. The behavior is intentional, not covert, so the risk is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Output Path Can Write Research Results
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The command-line interface accepts an output path and opens it for writing. This can overwrite files accessible to the process if a user or automation supplies a sensitive path, although no hidden write target is present. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: The write path comes from args.output and is passed directly to open for write mode.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is direct and user-controlled. It appears to be documented functionality and not malicious persistence or exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Steers Assistants Toward Extra Tools and Hosted Product
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill text tells assistants to generate scientific schematics by default for new documents and to suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows. This is behavioral steering outside the core research lookup purpose and should be reviewed for marketplace policy and user transparency. Confidence: 0.70. Confidence reasoning: The instructions are explicit, but they do not claim system authority or tell the model to bypass security review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavioral steering text is present and clear. It is promotional and cross-skill guidance rather than a sophisticated prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Is Mostly Legitimate Configuration
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for credential misuse. The code reads OPENROUTER_API_KEY only to authenticate OpenRouter requests and to print setup guidance when missing. No evidence found that the key is logged, printed, or sent to an unrelated destination. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: The key is used in the Authorization header for the declared API integration, with no other secret-handling path found in inspected source files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern is real, but semantic context confirms normal API-key configuration. I did not find evidence of credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Documentation Code Fences Were Misread as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command findings in SKILL.md are primarily markdown fenced examples that show how a user may run scripts. They are not executable code inside the skill runtime. Confidence: 0.89. Confidence reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples, not shell execution statements in source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The analyzer matched markdown backticks. The content is not parsed or executed by the Python scripts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The inspected code does not implement cryptography or use weak hash algorithms at the cited representative locations. The matches appear to come from words in model names, research text, or unrelated documentation. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: No cryptographic API usage was found in the relevant Python or markdown context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code handles research responses, citations, or documentation. I did not find MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Used with Outbound Network Request
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines environment-based API credentials with outbound requests to OpenRouter. This is expected for the integration, but it requires disclosure and careful handling of user query data. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The authorization header and requests.post call are directly visible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in code and semantically tied to a known external API service.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct File Write From CLI Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI opens the supplied output path in write mode. This is useful for saving results, but unsafe automation could overwrite local files. Confidence: 0.74. Confidence reasoning: args.output flows directly into open without path restriction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sink is direct, but the path is user-specified rather than hidden or remote-controlled.

content.value_statement: Research tasks often need current papers, source links, and clear citations. This skill queries scholarly sources through OpenRouter and formats results for literature review, verification, and synthesis.
actual_capabilities: Searches scholarly and technical sources through OpenRouter using Perplexity Sonar models.
Selects a fast lookup model or reasoning model based on query complexity.
Formats research summaries with source links, DOI references, and citation context.
Runs single or batch research queries from command-line Python scripts.
Can write human-readable or JSON-formatted research results to a chosen output file.
limitations: Requires an OPENROUTER_API_KEY and active OpenRouter account credits.
Sends research queries and context to an external API provider.
May miss paywalled papers, restricted databases, or newly indexed publications.
Citation details and source quality still require human verification before publication.

use_cases.title: Build a focused literature review
use_cases.description: Find recent papers, summarize major findings, and collect citation details for a manuscript section.

use_cases.title: Verify scientific claims
use_cases.description: Check statistics, methods, and research statements against current scholarly sources before sharing them.

use_cases.title: Compare research methods
use_cases.description: Use reasoning queries to compare approaches, explain tradeoffs, and identify research gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent papers
prompt_templates.prompt: Find recent academic papers on {topic} from {year_range}. Include citations, DOI links, and a short summary of each paper.

prompt_templates.title: Check a claim
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify this claim using current scholarly sources: {claim}. Include supporting and conflicting evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Compare methods
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare {method_one} and {method_two} for {use_case}. Cover evidence quality, limitations, and practical tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize a research area
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a structured synthesis of {research_area}. Prioritize landmark papers, recent reviews, controversies, and open questions.

output_examples.input: Find recent studies on CRISPR off-target effects in clinical trials.
output: A concise summary of recent clinical evidence.
A ranked list of relevant papers with journal names and dates.
Source links and DOI references where available.

output_examples.input: Compare transformer models and graph neural networks for molecular property prediction.
output: A side-by-side comparison of model strengths and limits.
Discussion of benchmark results and data requirements.
Research gaps and suggested follow-up sources.

output_examples.input: Verify a statistic about global renewable energy adoption in 2025.
output: A dated statistic with source attribution.
Notes on methodology and uncertainty when available.
Related sources for cross-checking the figure.

best_practices: Use specific topics, dates, populations, and study types in each query.
Review all cited papers directly before using them in formal work.
Avoid sending confidential unpublished research details to the external API.
anti_patterns: Do not ask broad questions like “Tell me about biology” and expect a useful literature review.
Do not treat generated citation lists as final without checking the primary sources.
Do not use the output path option in automation without controlling where files can be written.

faq.question: What API key does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs OPENROUTER_API_KEY set in the environment before live research queries can run.

faq.question: Does it keep research queries local?
faq.answer: No. The skill sends query text and formatted prompts to OpenRouter for processing.

faq.question: Which models does it use?
faq.answer: It uses Perplexity Sonar models through OpenRouter, with automatic selection for simple or complex queries.

faq.question: Can it create citations automatically?
faq.answer: It extracts source links, DOI references, and citation details when the API response includes them.

faq.question: Can it search paywalled databases?
faq.answer: It may find public metadata, but it cannot guarantee access to restricted full text.

faq.question: Is it safe for unpublished or confidential work?
faq.answer: Use caution. Do not send confidential data unless your policy allows sharing it with the external API provider.
